root_cause.text: The NFC subsystem fails to enforce a maximum length limit on the SDP URI, allowing an arbitrarily long URI to overflow the 8-bit `tlv_len` field and cause an out-of-bounds write during TLV construction in `nfc_llcp_build_sdreq_tlv`.
root_cause.evidence: The patch introduces a bounds check `if (WARN_ON_ONCE(uri_len > U8_MAX - 4))` and updates the netlink policy to limit `NFC_SDP_ATTR_URI` to `U8_MAX - 4`, with the commit message explicitly noting that "tlv_len is u8, so we need to limit the size of the SDP URI."

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The NFC subsystem fails to enforce a maximum length limit on the SDP URI, allowing an arbitrarily long URI to overflow the 8-bit `tlv_len` field and cause an out-of-bounds write during TLV construction in `nfc_llcp_build_sdreq_tlv`.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch introduces a bounds check `if (WARN_ON_ONCE(uri_len > U8_MAX - 4))` and updates the netlink policy to limit `NFC_SDP_ATTR_URI` to `U8_MAX - 4`, with the commit message explicitly noting that "tlv_len is u8, so we need to limit the size of the SDP URI."

intent_record.intent_summary: Add boundary check for uri_len to prevent integer overflow in tlv_len calculation.
intent_record.what: Added a boundary check for uri_len.
intent_record.how: Prevents integer overflow by ensuring the URI length plus overhead fits within an unsigned 8-bit integer.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add a length limit to the NFC SDP URI netlink attribute to guard against excessive input lengths.
intent_record.what: Added a maximum length constraint of U8_MAX - 4 to the NFC_SDP_ATTR_URI netlink attribute policy.
intent_record.how: Restricts the maximum allowable length for the parsed URI string, preventing the processing of overly long inputs.

grouping_result.intent: Add a length limit to the NFC SDP URI netlink attribute to guard against excessive input lengths.

consistency_review.core_intent: Add length and boundary checks for the NFC SDP URI netlink attribute to prevent integer overflows and excessive input lengths.

relevance_review.reason: The group directly addresses the vulnerability by adding the missing length limit and boundary checks for the SDP URI, preventing the integer overflow in the tlv_len calculation as described in the root cause.

candidates.rationale: This line defines the netlink policy for the SDP URI attribute without specifying a maximum length, which allows an arbitrarily long URI to be passed from user space, causing the vulnerability.
This is the point where the TLV structure allocation begins without previously verifying if the uri_len exceeds the 8-bit limit.

vuln_statements.explanation: This defines the netlink policy for the SDP URI attribute without specifying a maximum length, allowing an arbitrarily long URI to be passed from user space which leads to the overflow.
This initiates the processing of the URI without a prior length check, missing the opportunity to validate the uri_len before the 8-bit limit overflow.

bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit d9b8d8e19b073096d3609bbd60f82148d128b555
Author: Thierry Escande <thierry.escande@linux.intel.com>
Date:   Fri Feb 15 10:43:06 2013 +0100

    NFC: llcp: Service Name Lookup netlink interface
    
    This adds a netlink interface for service name lookup support.
    Multiple URIs can be passed nested into the NFC_ATTR_LLC_SDP attribute
    using the NFC_CMD_LLC_SDREQ netlink command.
    When the SNL reply is received, a NFC_EVENT_LLC_SDRES event is sent to
    the user space. URI and SA
bic_trace_step.reason: The function nfc_genl_llc_sdreq parses the URI length 'uri_len = nla_len(sdp_attrs[NFC_SDP_ATTR_URI]);' and passes it directly to 'nfc_llcp_build_sdreq_tlv(tid, uri, uri_len);' without enforcing any maximum length limit. This allows an arbitrarily long URI to be processed, leading to the vulnerability.

bic_trace_step.reason: The vulnerability pertains to `nfc_llcp_build_sdreq_tlv` and SDP URI length handling, which are part of the LLCP protocol implementation. This commit only introduces the generic netlink interface for NFC (`net/nfc/netlink.c`) and does not contain any of the vulnerable logic.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
NFC: llcp: Service Name Lookup netlink interface

This adds a netlink interface for service name lookup support.
Multipl
bic_trace_step.reason: The function allocates and builds the SDREQ TLV using `uri_len` without validating that it does not exceed the maximum allowed length (which would prevent overflowing the 8-bit length fields). Specifically, there is no check on `uri_len` before `sdreq->tlv_len = uri_len + 3;`.
